message:
feat(core,abi): port ns_name_unpack + ns_name_pack DNS codec to core::resolv::dns_name (bd-nsu-epic)

Lifts ~265 lines of inline RFC 1035 DNS message codec (compression-
pointer-following decompression + uncompressed packing) across 4 abi
entry points (ns_name_unpack, __ns_name_unpack, ns_name_pack,
__ns_name_pack) into the existing core::resolv::dns_name module.

New core surface (in resolv::dns_name):
  pub const NS_CMPRSFLGS: u8 = 0xC0;
  pub const NS_MAX_JUMPS: u32 = 128;
  pub fn name_unpack(msg: &[u8], src_offset: usize, dst: &mut [u8])
      -> Result<usize, NameError>
  pub fn name_pack(src: &[u8], dst: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize, NameError>

name_unpack follows compression pointers from src_offset within the
bounded msg slice, returning bytes consumed from the original src
position (caller-cursor advance). The 128-jump limit defends against
malicious circular pointer chains.

name_pack walks length-prefixed labels and copies them verbatim — no
compression dictionary lookup (matches current abi behavior).

abi changes (in glibc_internal_abi.rs):
  - 4 inline impls (~265 lines combined) replaced with thin shims
    that materialize msg/eom/src/dst as byte slices and call core.
  - Drop orphan const NS_MAXLABEL (now in core).

Combined with bd-nsn-epic, the entire ns_name_* family (8 entry
points: ntop/pton/unpack/pack each with __glibc-internal duplicate)
now delegates to a single set of core helpers.

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-nsu-2):
  - 14 new core::resolv::dns_name unit tests pass (incl. circular-
    pointer defense, src-outside-msg rejection, unpack(pack(msg))
    round-trip property)
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - 29/29 ns_name integration tests pass (including the compression-
    follow tests ns_name_unpack_with_compression and
    ns_name_unpack_decompresses_wire_name)
  - cargo clippy -p core -p abi -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-nsu-epic.
